data.title: BTCS, a US-listed company, will raise $57.8 million to hoard ETH
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Blockchain technology company BTCS (NASDAQ: BTCS) announced on Wednesday that it has reached an agreement with ATW Partners to raise up to $57.8 million through convertible bonds, all of which will be used to acquire Ethereum (ETH). The first $7.8 million bond has been issued, and the remaining $50 million will be launched in batches according to the negotiation between the two parties.
BTCS Chairman and CEO Charles Allen said: "We are following MicroStrategy's strategy of accumulating Bitcoin and systematically increasing Ethereum holdings."
It is reported that BTCS mainly operates Ethereum/BNBChain verification nodes, crypto asset staking and on-chain data analysis business.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs had a total net inflow of $320 million yesterday, and none of the twelve ETFs had a net outflow
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (May 14, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was US$320 million.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Blackrock ETF IBIT, with a single-day net inflow of US$233 million. Currently, IBIT's total net inflow has reached US$45.013 billion.
The second is Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a single-day net inflow of US$36.1349 million. Currently, FBTC's total net inflow has reached US$11.651 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is US$121.845 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Bitcoin's total market value) is 5.92%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$41.399 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETF had a total net inflow of $63.4699 million yesterday, and none of the nine ETFs had a net outflow
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (May 14, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Ethereum spot ETFs was $63.4699 million.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was Blackrock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $57.6085 million per day. Currently, ETHA's total net inflow has reached $4.255 billion.
The second is Fidelity ETF FETH, with a net inflow of $5.8614 million per day. Currently, FETH's total net inflow has reached $1.425 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $9.04 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Ethereum's total market value) is 2.88%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $2.529 billion.

data.title: Japan’s carmakers are expecting $19 billion in losses from US tariffs
data.description:
Japan’s biggest carmakers expect to lose more than $19 billion after the tariffs that United States President Donald Trump placed on imported vehicles and parts sent shockwaves through the auto industry. The industry’s leading brands say the duties will squeeze financial performance this year and beyond. Some firms have even stopped giving guidance while they […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/japans-carmakers-19-billion-loss-tariffs/

data.title: Hong Kong Enhances Security Measures for Virtual Asset Ecosystem
data.description: According to PANews, the Hong Kong Police Force's Cyber Security and Technology Crime Bureau has released an update on the security maintenance of the city's virtual asset ecosystem. Chief Superintendent Lam Cheuk-ho emphasized the shared responsibility among exchanges, regulatory bodies, and law enforcement agencies in ensuring security. The bureau has collaborated with the University of Hong Kong to develop CryptoTrace, a virtual asset analysis tool designed to provide advanced intelligence and investigative support for crime investigation units. Additionally, a new anti-fraud team has been established in the New Territories South region.
